title: Show active filter in Beads panel header
description: Update the Beads list panel header to include the currently active filter, e.g. 'Beads [Ready]' or 'Beads [Blocked]', and keep it in sync when filter mode changes.
acceptance_criteria: Beads panel header includes active filter label.
Header updates immediately when cycling filters.
Regression tests cover header text for at least default and one status-specific filter.

handoff_summary.completed: Implemented a shared filter-label/title formatter in `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py`, passed the active filter into `render_tree_panel`, and updated the Textual list panel border title so the header renders as `Beads [Default] [ACTIVE]` / `Beads [Ready] [ACTIVE]` as filters change. Added tests in `tests/test_tui.py` covering default and blocked header text plus an app-level filter-cycling check that verifies the header updates to `Ready`.
handoff_summary.risks: Low conflict risk if sibling work is also changing Beads panel title rendering in `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py` or nearby TUI regression assertions in `tests/test_tui.py`. Display labels are derived by title-casing filter ids, so any future non-humanized filter ids may need an explicit mapping.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Potential overlap with any concurrent bead editing Beads panel header rendering or TUI header assertions in `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py` and `tests/test_tui.py`.

conflict_risks: Potential overlap with any concurrent bead editing Beads panel header rendering or TUI header assertions in `src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py` and `tests/test_tui.py`.

Updated the Beads panel header to show the active filter label and keep it in sync across both the plain-text renderer and the Textual panel border title. Added regression coverage for default and status-specific headers, including filter cycling.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Run targeted validation needed to confirm the implementation.

Disallowed actions:
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
